title: Running startProject via shiny app
text: can also launch local Shiny app facilitate using startProject(): running RStudio, app appear pane viewer can change browser choice (see documentation details).
code: runApp_startProject()

title: Running startProject via shiny app
text: ease use, can create executable shortcut using shinyShortcut package. create .shiny_run folder containing executable file location application files. can double click file run app default browser. easier access executable file, can copy rename file location choice.
code: # install.packages("shinyShortcut") shinyShortcut::shinyShortcut(     shinyDirectory = system.file("application", package = "startProject"),      OS = "windows", gitIgnore=TRUE) file.copy(     paste0(system.file("application", package = "startProject"), "/.shiny_run/shinyShortCut.cmd"),      "C:/Users/userid/Desktop",     overwrite = TRUE)       file.rename(     "C:/Users/userid/Desktop/shinyShortCut.cmd",      "C:/Users/userid/Desktop/startProject.cmd")

title: Getting started with startProject
text: can install development version GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("CU-Anschutz-DOS-CORP/startProject") library(startProject)

title: Getting started with startProject
text: can create project directory startProject() launching local Shiny App included package.

title: Getting started with startProject
text: startProject() allows input project information specify sub-folders want included. addition, can request creation memo, R, Rmd /SAS templates. templates requested, function search memo, rcode, r, sascode sas sub-folders store corresponding template; found, templates sub-folder created. directory created look like : Legacy Structure Snap Shot Structure project information (client, author, etc.) template details (purpose, notes) used populate templates. templates created can viewed .
code: startProject(   main.dir = Sys.getenv("HOME"),    proj.name = "TestRProject",    start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),    version = "1",    client = "John Smith",   client.dept = "Testing Department",    main.statistician = "Rocio Lopez",    structure = "snapshot"   memo.name = NULL,    memo.re = "Get started with startProject",   r.name = NULL,    r.purpose = "This R file displays the template generated by startProject",    r.notes = NULL,   rmd.name = NULL,   rmd.purpose =  "This Rmd file displays the template generated by startProject",    rmd.notes = NULL,   sas.name = NULL,    sas.purpose =  "This SAS file displays the template generated by startProject",    sas.notes = NULL) [Project_Root]/ ├── communications ├── data                       <-- .sas7bdat and .rds work files ├── graphs                     <-- PDF/PNG visual outputs ├── memo                       <-- Written report for PI ├── orig_data                  <-- Specific data pull/Chart review data ├── others ├── r                          <-- R code files ├── temp │ └── sas                        <-- SAS code files     └── _include/              <-- <-- Project-specific SAS Macros, functions, etc. [Project_Root]/ ├── [Study_Name].Rproj          <-- If using R Projects ├── 00_protocol_and_irb/        <-- IRB, DUA, and Study Protocol ├── 01_data_specs/              <-- Data dictionaries and coding crosswalks ├── 02_docs/                    <-- Your structural definitions and project logic ├── 03_include/                 <-- Project-specific SAS Macros, R Functions, and Formats ├── 04_manuscript/              <-- Drafts and reviewer responses ├── 05_presentations/           <-- Conference slides and posters ├── 06_external_resources/      <-- Relevant literature docs ├── README.md                   <-- Master Project Guide │ └── analysis_2026-04-27/        <-- Version 1 (V1)     ├── orig_data/              <-- Specific data pull/Chart review data     ├── code/                   <-- 00_setup.sas, 01_clean.sas, 02_viz.R     ├── data/                   <-- .sas7bdat and .rds work files     ├── output_raw/             <-- Unedited output and tables     ├── graphs/                 <-- PDF/PNG visual outputs     ├── final_styled/           <-- Manually edited/formatted results     └── memo/                   <-- Written report for PI

title: Getting started with startProject
text: prefer, can launch local Shiny app facilitate using startProject(). details app create short cut allows accessing startProject app without opening R, go .

title: Getting started with startProject
text: package may utilize several document templates can customized: memoTemplate.docx basefile.R multi_setup_basefile.R, multi_data_management_baserfile.R, multi_analysis_basefile.R, layout = “multi” basefile.Rmd basefile.sas, layout = “single” multi_setup_basefile.sas, multi_data_management_baserfile.sas, multi_analysis_basefile.sas, layout = “multi” basefile.md, structure = “snapshot” use customized templates can either modify included save copies home directory (details ).

title: Getting started with startProject
text: header layout memoTemplate.docx can modified important change names placeholders information passed functions (DATESEC, TOSEC, etc.); want omit information can delete placeholder. can save modified template home directory (Sys.getenv("HOME")) name .basefile.docx (note . filename); basefile override included template future installation package. can also save .staffInfo file home directory (Sys.getenv("HOME")) name, email phone number inserted memo footer. Leave last line blank avoid warning message R. file look like :

title: Getting started with startProject
text: headers .R, .Rmd .sas generated startProject functions changed. can append code comments generated header either creating .basefile (e.g., .basefile.R, .basefile.Rmd .basefile.sas) home directory (Sys.getenv("HOME")) modifying base file included package (instructions next section); allows add commonly used code templates. base files included package note indicating can modify ; wish generate template(s) header, simply delete files clear current content.

title: Getting started with startProject
text: can physically delete open modify template files included package. find location included template files, use system.file("templates", package = "startProject"). Note names files changed. tasks can done within R session. delete .R template file, example, can use: already templates stored separate location, can move files using file.copy(). example: template file name package template, overwrite existing template . template different file name, must rename . overwrite existing template . strongly recommended .basefiles home directory store copies modified templates separate location use functions move files package directory; facilitate package updates.
code: unlink(paste0(system.file("templates", package = "startProject"), "/basefile.R")) file.copy(   "C:/FromDirectory",    system.file("templates", package = "startProject"), overwrite = TRUE) file.rename(   paste0(system.file("templates", package = "startProject"), "/YourTemplateNameHere.R"),    paste0(system.file("templates", package = "startProject"), "/basefile.R"))

text: Rocio Lopez. Author, maintainer.

what: Create a Project Directory and Templates
title: Create a Project Directory and Templates
text: goal startProject create project directory sub-folders, document templates (.docx, .R, .Rmd, .sas). templates populated project information provide. package also contains set functions allow create additional templates needed.

title: Create a Project Directory and Templates
text: can install development version startProject GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("CU-Anschutz-DOS-CORP/startProject") library(startProject)

title: Create a Project Directory and Templates
text: details get started startProject go .

title: Create a .docx template — makeMemoTemplate
text: `makeMemoTemplate()` generate .docx file used project     memos. document header project information provided     function call.

title: Create a .docx template — makeMemoTemplate
code: makeMemoTemplate(   memo.dir = getwd(),   memo.name = NULL,   start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),   version = "1",   client = NULL,   client.dept = NULL,   main.statistician = NULL,   stats.collab = NULL,   memo.re = NULL )

title: Create a .docx template — makeMemoTemplate
text: memo.dir character string specifying file path template stored. Defaults getwd(). memo.name NULL character string specifying name .Rmd file. NULL, file named memo[currentdate]_v[version] memo[currentdate]. start.date NULL character string providing date included template header. Defaults current date. version NULL character string providing project version included template name /header. Defaults "1". client NULL character string providing name(s) client(s) included template header. client.dept NULL character string providing affiliation client(s) included template header. main.statistician NULL character string providing name primary statistician. used template header. stats.collab NULL character string providing additional collaborators included authors template header. memo.re NULL character string providing subject line memo template.

title: Create a .docx template — makeMemoTemplate
text: can modify memo template either creating .basefile.docx file    HOME directory modifying templates/memoTemplate    contained package library. Note text use placeholders    passed information (DATESEC, TOSEC, etc.) changed. find location HOME directory, run     `Sys.getenv("HOME")`. find location included template file, run     `system.file("templates", package = "startProject")`.

title: Create a .docx template — makeMemoTemplate
text: Rocio Lopez, Rocio.LopezMoscoso@cuanschutz.edu

title: Create a .docx template — makeMemoTemplate
code: if (FALSE) makeMemoTemplate(memo.name = "memoTemplate") # \dontrun{}

title: Create a .Rmd template — makeRmdTemplate
text: `makRmdTemplate` generate .Rmd file header containing    project information provided function call.

title: Create a .Rmd template — makeRmdTemplate
code: makeRmdTemplate(   rmd.dir = getwd(),   rmd.name = NULL,   rmd.output = "word",   proj.name = NULL,   start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),   version = "1",   client = NULL,   client.dept = NULL,   main.statistician = NULL,   stats.collab = NULL,   rmd.purpose = NULL,   rmd.notes = NULL )

title: Create a .Rmd template — makeRmdTemplate
text: rmd.dir character string specifying file path template stored. Defaults getwd(). rmd.name NULL character string specifying name .Rmd file. NULL, file named p[proj.name]_rmd[currentdate]_v[version] rmd[currentdate]. rmd.output character string specifying type Rmarkdown output created. Choices : word, html pdf. Defaults word. proj.name NULL character string providing name project included template name /header. start.date NULL character string providing date included template header. Defaults today's date. version NULL character string providing project version included template name /header. Defaults "1". client NULL character string providing name(s) client(s) included template header. client.dept NULL character string providing affiliation client(s) included template header. main.statistician NULL character string providing name primary statistician. used template header. stats.collab NULL character string providing additional collaborators included authors template header. rmd.purpose NULL character string providing purpose generated template included template header. rmd.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included template header.

title: Create a .Rmd template — makeRmdTemplate
text: can append code comments generated header either    creating .basefile.Rmd file /home/<USERNAME> directory modifying     templates/basefile.Rmd file contained package library. find location HOME directory, run     `Sys.getenv("HOME")`. find location included template file, run     `system.file("templates", package = "startProject")`.

title: Create a .Rmd template — makeRmdTemplate
text: Rocio Lopez, Rocio.LopezMoscoso@cuanschutz.edu

title: Create a .Rmd template — makeRmdTemplate
code: if (FALSE) makeRmdTemplate(rmd.name = "rmdTemplate") # \dontrun{}

title: Create a .R template — makeRTemplate
text: `makeRTemplate` generate .R file header containing    project information provided function call.

title: Create a .R template — makeRTemplate
code: makeRTemplate(   r.dir = getwd(),   r.name = NULL,   proj.name = NULL,   start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),   version = "1",   client = NULL,   client.dept = NULL,   main.statistician = NULL,   stats.collab = NULL,   r.purpose = NULL,   r.notes = NULL,   r.template.layout = c("single", "multi"),   r.header.style = c("default", "simple") )

title: Create a .R template — makeRTemplate
text: r.dir character string specifying file path template stored. Defaults getwd(). r.name NULL character string specifying name .R file. NULL, file named p[proj.name]_r[currentdate]_v[version] r[currentdate]. proj.name NULL character string providing name project included template name /header. start.date NULL character string providing date included template header. Defaults today's date. version NULL character string providing project version included template name /header. Defaults "1". client NULL character string providing name(s) client(s) included template header. client.dept NULL character string providing affiliation client(s) included template header. main.statistician NULL character string providing name primary statistician. used template header. stats.collab NULL character string providing additional collaborators included authors template header. r.purpose NULL character string providing purpose generated template included template header. r.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included template header. r.template.layout character string specifying R template layout. Supported values "single" "multi". Defaults "single". r.header.style character string specifying R header style. Supported values "default" "simple". Defaults "default".

title: Create a .R template — makeRTemplate
text: can append code comments generated header either    creating .basefile.R file /home/<USERNAME> directory modifying     templates/basefile.R file contained package library. find location HOME directory, run     `Sys.getenv("HOME")`. find location included template file, run     `system.file("templates", package = "startProject")`.

title: Create a .R template — makeRTemplate
text: Rocio Lopez, Rocio.LopezMoscoso@cuanschutz.edu

title: Create a .R template — makeRTemplate
code: if (FALSE) makeRTemplate(r.name = "rTemplate") # \dontrun{}

title: Create a .sas template — makeSasTemplate

title: Create a .sas template — makeSasTemplate
code: makeSasTemplate(   sas.dir = getwd(),   sas.name = NULL,   proj.name = NULL,   start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),   version = "1",   client = NULL,   client.dept = NULL,   main.statistician = NULL,   stats.collab = NULL,   sas.purpose = NULL,   sas.notes = NULL,   sas.template.layout = c("single", "multi"),   sas.header.style = c("default", "simple") )

title: Create a .sas template — makeSasTemplate
text: sas.dir character string specifying file path template stored. Defaults getwd(). sas.name NULL character string specifying name .sas file. NULL, file named p[proj.name]_sas[currentdate]_v[version] sas[currentdate]. proj.name NULL character string providing name project included template name /header. start.date NULL character string providing date included template header. Defaults today's date. version NULL character string providing project version included template name /header. Defaults "1". client NULL character string providing name(s) client(s) included template header. client.dept NULL character string providing affiliation client(s) included template header. main.statistician NULL character string providing name primary statistician. used template header. stats.collab NULL character string providing additional collaborators included authors template header. sas.purpose NULL character string providing purpose generated template included template header. sas.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included template header. sas.template.layout character string specifying SAS template layout. Supported values "single" "multi". Defaults "single". sas.header.style character string specifying SAS header style. Supported values "default" "simple". Defaults "default".

title: Create a .sas template — makeSasTemplate
text: can append code comments generated header either    creating .basefile.sas file /home/<USERNAME> directory modifying     templates/basefile.sas file contained package library. find location HOME directory, run     `Sys.getenv("HOME")`. find location included template file, run     `system.file("templates", package = "startProject")`.

what: Create a snapshot-style project structure analysis subfolder — makeSnapshot
title: Create a snapshot-style project structure analysis subfolder — makeSnapshot
text: `makeSnapshot()` creates dated analysis snapshot folder, template files     placed snapshot code directory.

title: Create a snapshot-style project structure analysis subfolder — makeSnapshot
code: makeSnapshot(   project.dir = getwd(),   proj.name = NULL,   proj.num = NULL,   start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),   version = "1",   client = NULL,   client.dept = NULL,   main.statistician = NULL,   stats.collab = NULL,   templates = "memo, R, Rmd, sas",   memo.name = NULL,   memo.re = NULL,   r.name = NULL,   r.purpose = NULL,   r.notes = NULL,   rmd.name = NULL,   rmd.purpose = NULL,   rmd.notes = NULL,   sas.name = NULL,   sas.purpose = NULL,   sas.notes = NULL,   snapshot.name = NULL,   analysis.subfolders = c("orig_data", "code", "data", "output_raw", "graphs",     "final_styled", "memo"),   sas.template.layout = c("single", "multi"),   r.template.layout = c("single", "multi"),   sas.header.style = c("default", "simple"),   r.header.style = c("default", "simple") )

title: Create a snapshot-style project structure analysis subfolder — makeSnapshot
text: project.dir character string specifying path project root created. proj.name NULL character string providing name project. proj.num NULL character string providing project number. start.date NULL character string providing date included templates. Defaults today's date. version NULL character string providing project version included templates. Defaults "1". client NULL character string providing name(s) client(s). client.dept NULL character string providing affiliation client(s). main.statistician NULL character string providing name primary statistician. stats.collab NULL character string providing additional collaborators. templates character string providing comma delimited list templates generated. Valid options memo, R, Rmd sas. memo.name NULL character string specifying name .docx memo template. memo.re NULL character string providing subject line memo template. r.name NULL character string specifying name .R template. r.purpose NULL character string providing purpose R file included R template header. r.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included R template header. rmd.name NULL character string specifying name .Rmd template. rmd.purpose NULL character string providing purpose Rmd file included Rmd template header. rmd.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included Rmd template header. sas.name NULL character string specifying name .sas template. sas.purpose NULL character string providing purpose SAS file included template header. sas.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included SAS template header. snapshot.name NULL character string providing snapshot folder name. NULL, analysis_YYYYMMDD folder created. analysis.subfolders character vector providing subfolders create inside analysis snapshot directory. Defaults standard Snapshot layout: orig_data, code, data, output_raw, graphs, final_styled, memo. sas.template.layout character string specifying SAS template layout use Snapshot projects. Supported values "single" "multi". Defaults "single". r.template.layout character string specifying R template layout use Snapshot projects. Supported values "single" "multi". Defaults "single". sas.header.style character string specifying SAS header style. Supported values "default" "simple". Defaults "default". r.header.style character string specifying R header style. Supported values "default" "simple". Defaults "default".

title: Launch startProject Shiny App — runApp_startProject
text: `runApp_startProject` open viewer window allow     run startProject local shiny App.

title: Launch startProject Shiny App — runApp_startProject
code: runApp_startProject(browser = "WindowViewer")

title: Launch startProject Shiny App — runApp_startProject
text: browser character string specifies browser app launched. Valid options "PaneViewer" run RStudio Viewer tab, "WindowViewer" run RStudio Window "WindowExternal" run default web browser (case ignored). Defaults "WindowViewer".

title: Launch startProject Shiny App — runApp_startProject
text: browser argument takes effect running within RStudio.

title: Launch startProject Shiny App — runApp_startProject
text: Rocio Lopez, lopezr@ccf.org

title: Launch startProject Shiny App — runApp_startProject
code: if (FALSE) runApp_startProject(browser = "PaneViewer") # \dontrun{}

what: startProject: A package for creating a project directory — startProject-package
title: startProject: A package for creating a project directory — startProject-package
text: start.project package can used create project directory generate memo code templates.

title: startProject: A package for creating a project directory — startProject-package
text: Functions create project directory sub-folders templates: startProject: function create project directory. runApp_startProject: function launch startProject     local Shiny app. Functions create additional templates: makeMemoTemplate: function generate .docx memo template. makeRmdTemplate: function generate .Rmd template. makeRTemplate: function generate .R template. makeSasTemplate: function generate .sas template.

title: startProject: A package for creating a project directory — startProject-package
text: link web page/tutorial .

title: startProject: A package for creating a project directory — startProject-package

what: Create a project directory with sub-folders and templates — startProject
title: Create a project directory with sub-folders and templates — startProject
text: `startProject()` create project directory along specified    sub-folders optional .docx, .R, .Rmd .sas templates containing    headers project information.

title: Create a project directory with sub-folders and templates — startProject
code: startProject(   main.dir = getwd(),   proj.name = "project name",   proj.num = NULL,   start.date = format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y"),   version = "1",   client = NULL,   client.dept = NULL,   main.statistician = NULL,   stats.collab = NULL,   subfolders = "communications, data, graphs, memo, orig_data, others, r, sas, temp",   templates = "memo, R, Rmd, sas",   structure = c("legacy", "snapshot"),   analysis.subfolders = c("orig_data", "code", "data", "output_raw", "graphs",     "final_styled", "memo"),   sas.template.layout = c("single", "multi"),   r.template.layout = c("single", "multi"),   sas.header.style = c("default", "simple"),   r.header.style = c("default", "simple"),   memo.name = NULL,   memo.re = NULL,   r.name = NULL,   r.purpose = NULL,   r.notes = NULL,   rmd.name = NULL,   rmd.purpose = NULL,   rmd.notes = NULL,   sas.name = NULL,   sas.purpose = NULL,   sas.notes = NULL )

title: Create a project directory with sub-folders and templates — startProject
text: main.dir character string specifying file path project directory created. Defaults getwd(). proj.name NULL character string providing name project included templates. proj.num NULL character string providing project number directory name templates. NULL, [proj.name] used instead. start.date NULL character string providing date included templates. Defaults today's date. version NULL character string providing project version included templates. Defaults "1". client NULL character string providing name(s) client(s) included templates. client.dept NULL character string providing affiliation client(s) included templates. main.statistician NULL character string providing name primary statistician. used templates. stats.collab NULL character string providing additional collaborators included authors templates. subfolders character string providing comma delimited list sub-folders created. Defaults "communications, data, graphs, memo, orig_data, others, r, sas, temp". templates character string providing comma delimited list templates generated. Valid options memo, R, Rmd sas. Defaults "memo, R, Rmd, sas". structure character string specifying project layout create. Supported values "legacy" "snapshot". Defaults "legacy". analysis.subfolders character vector providing subfolders create inside analysis snapshot directory. Defaults standard Snapshot layout: orig_data, code, data, output_raw, graphs, final_styled, memo. sas.template.layout character string specifying SAS template layout. Supported values "single" "multi". Defaults "single". r.template.layout character string specifying R template layout. Supported values "single" "multi". Defaults "single". sas.header.style character string specifying SAS header style. Supported values "default" "simple". Defaults "default". r.header.style character string specifying R header style. Supported values "default" "simple". Defaults "default". memo.name NULL character string specifying name .Rmd file. NULL, file named p[proj.num]_memo[currentdate]_v[version] p[proj.num]_memo[currentdate]. memo.re NULL character string providing subject line memo template. r.name NULL character string specifying name .R template. NULL, file named p[proj.num]_r[currentdate]_v[version] p[proj.num]_r[currentdate]. r.purpose NULL character string providing purpose R file included R template header. r.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included R template header. rmd.name NULL character string specifying name .Rmd template. NULL, file named p[proj.num]_rmd[currentdate]_v[version] p[proj.num]_rmd_[currentdate] rmd.purpose NULL character string providing purpose Rmd file included Rmd template header. rmd.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included Rmd template header. sas.name NULL character string specifying name .sas file. NULL, file named p[proj.num]_sas[currentdate]_v[version] p[proj.num]_sas[currentdate]. sas.purpose NULL character string providing purpose SAS file included template header. sas.notes NULL character string providing additional notes included SAS template header.

title: Create a project directory with sub-folders and templates — startProject
text: main directory, sub-folders /templates exist, function     generate warning message continue create components     exist. Templates memo, sas, r Rmd (case ignored) supported.     templates requested, function search memo, rcode     r, sascode sas sub-folders store templates;     found, templates sub-folder created. can modify memo template either creating .basefile.docx     file HOME directory modifying templates/memoTemplate     contained package library. Note text use placeholders     passed information (DATESEC, TOSEC, etc.) changed. can also append code comments generated header     code files (.R, .Rmd, .sas) either creating .basefile.R, .basefile.Rmd     /.basefile.sas file(s) HOME directory modifying     templates/basefile.xxx file contained package library. find location HOME directory, run     `Sys.getenv("HOME")`. find location included template files, run     `system.file("templates", package = "startProject")`.
